title: Dengue virus serotype 2 NS2B-NS3 protease fusion construct small scale expression and purification for biophysical and biochemical assays protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f7euh","text":"This protocol details the co-expression and purification of Dengue virus serotype 2 NS2B-NS3 protease fusion construct bearing a N-terminal His-StrepII tag at small scale (\u003c6L). The final product does not have its affinity tags removed, and is catalytically active. Final product is suitable for biophysical and biochemical assays. In the current version we added the Addgene id for the plasmid used and corrected the affiliation.\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: The construct encodes the NS2B and NS3 protease linked with a non-cleavable flexible linker, with a N-terminal His6-StrepII tag fusion. It was inoculated from its BL21(DE3)-RR glycerol stock. Plasmid bellow. DENV-2 Thailand/16681/84 NS2B-NS3 protease fusion (non-cleavable linker) addgene Catalog #228650 The DENV-2 NS2B-NS3 fusion construct encodes the NS2B and NS3 protease with a N-terminal His6-StrepII tag fusion on a kanamycin resistant plasmid backbone with a T7 promoter.
When the OD 600 approximately 1.8, add 1 millimolar (mM) IPTG. Lower the temperature and shaker speed to 180 rpm, 18 °C, . Incubate overnight.
Perform IMAC to extract target protein from the lysed cell mixture

Wash the column with 10 CV of base buffer, followed by 5 CV of wash buffer twice. Allow wash buffer to pass through completely between washes. This is to remove non-specific, weak binding of contaminant proteins from the resin for a cleaner elution. Collect washes separately for SDS-PAGE analysis.

Using the AKTA Pure system: Inject the sample onto a 5mL sample loop. Run the sample down HiLoad 16/60 Superdex 75 pg gel filtration column at 1mL/min in gel filtration buffer, collecting 1mL aliquots.
Aliquot into appropriate volumes for future usage to minimise freeze/thaw cycles. Flash-freeze in liquid nitrogen, and store at -80 °C until required.

Use 10 mL starter culture to inoculate every 1 L TB media (see Materials) supplemented with 50 ug/mL kanamycin and 8 mL 50% glycerol in a baffled flask. 200 rpm, 37 °C, For this protocol 4L of pellet was grown for purification
Harvest the cell by centrifugation at 4000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes . Discard supernatant and store pellet by freezing at -80 °C . 4L of culture was grown for this protocol.
Lyse cell pellet
Lyse by sonication 00:00:05, On 00:00:10, Off for a total 'on' time of 00:04:00 at 40% amplitude to fully rupture the cells. Ensure pellet is °C during sonication to prevent overheating.

Pour clarified lysate onto the the gravity flow column, and allow it to flow though over the washed resin. Collect FT separately for SDS-PAGE analysis. For SDS-PAGE samples, mix 15uL sample with 5uL 4x sample buffer, supplemented with 10mM DTT.
Elute the protein with 2.5 CV of elution buffer.
Repeat step 8.5 three more times, collecting a total of 4 separate elution fractions. This is to ensure maximum retrieval of protein from the resin.

Scrape off some of the glycerol stock with a sterile loop and use this to inoculate 2x 50 mL falcon tube containing 20 mL of LB each, supplemented with 50 ug/mL kanamycin. Grow the starter culture at 37 °C Overnight with 200 rpm shaking.
CV - column volume, total volume of resin in a column IMAC - immobilised metal affinity chromatography FT - flow through DVNS2B3 - DENV2 NS2B-NS3 protease

Run SDS-PAGE of all samples from total lysis supernatant to final elution. Stain gel with protein staining solution Coomasssie Blue and determine which fractions contain the target protein by finding the band corresponding to the target molecular weight. SDS-PAGE analysis of IMAC fractions shows target protein as the major species in wash 2 and the first two elution. Significant amount of target is present in wash 2, which suggest that the binding interaction between target and Ni resin is not very strong. The target protein is expected to be present mostly in the elution samples, although small amounts may be found in the FT and washes. If that is not the case, then further troubleshooting is required.
Purify sample further by size exclusion chromatography .

Dispense 2 mL Nickle affinity resin Ni Sepharose 6 FF - Cytiva into a gravity flow column. Equilibrate resin by first rinsing with ~ 10 CV distilled water, then ~ 10 CV binding buffer to remove the storage solution.
Centrifuge the lysed cells for 38000 x g, 4 °C, 1 hour to remove insoluble cell debris, and collect supernatant in a bottle 4 °C

See Materials tab for buffer compositions. DENV2 NS2B-NS3 His6-StrepII fusion protein properties Before tag cleavage: MW = 31543.04 Da E (assume all Cys reduced)= 54430 mM-1cm-1 PI = 5.49 After tag cleavage: MW = 25849.03 Da E (assume all Cys reduced)= 41940 mM-1cm-1 PI = 5.01 These values are determined by Expasy ProtParam Thaw and resuspend the pellet in ~7mL of lysis buffer per g of pellet. Stir gently with magnetic stir bar at Room temperature for 00:30:00 to allow lysozyme and bezonase to start breaking down cell components.
Using 10,000 MWCO spin concentrators, concentrate the rIMAC step containing fractions of the target protein to a final volume of under 5 mL .

Remove any solid aggregates from the sample by centrifugation at 17200 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes , then immediately draw up the supernatant with a 5mL syringe and a blunt-tip fill needle, taking care not to disturb the pellet. This is to remove as much solid particles from the injection sample as possible, so as to not clog the in-line filter or frit of the column. This can also be done by passing the sample through a 0.22 micron syringe filter.

From the chromatogram, fraction A7-B12 were analysed by SDS-PAGE. Chromatogram and SEC fraction SDS-PAGE analysis. Fractions A7-B12 were analysed on SDS-PAGE to determine which contained the target protein. Fractions B5-B9 were pooled as they contain mostly target protein in comparison to contaminants.

Take the fractions that contain the target protein, which in this case are fraction E8-F10. Concentrate the final sample in Vivaspin 15 10kda MWCO centrifugal concentrator until the concentration reaches > 7 mg/mL . Take 1 µL of the final sample for SDS-PAGE, which is not carried out here. Another 1 µL can be taken for mass spectroscopy (MS) analysis. Intact MS of the final sample. This confirms the uncleaved target protein is the major species in the final sample.
Add 5% PEI to lysate until final concentration of 0.15 % volume total lysate. This precipitates DNA, which can be removed by centrifugation in the next step.
creator: korvus.wang
